title: The European Cultural Model and the New World Order
summary: The article argues that Europe's strength lies in its 2,000-year-old cultural and democratic heritage, rooted in Greek and Roman civilization. Facing a new world order marked by authoritarian leaders in Russia, America, and China, the author contends that small countries like Romania can only thrive through EU unity, and calls for a European army and an executive EU presidency to respond rapidly to crises.
talking_points: The world has undergone a deep, permanent, and systemic rupture since 2020, not merely a transition
Sovereigntist arguments are fundamentally flawed because no small European country can negotiate alone with superpowers like Russia, China, or the USA
Europe needs to create a unified military force and an executive EU presidency capable of rapid crisis decision-making
Rising extremism across Europe is directly linked to the authoritarian model promoted by Russian and American leaders
The European democratic model, rooted in 2,000 years of civilization, has always prevailed over authoritarianism in the long run
author: Adrian Nița
